data.title: Frencken Group profit rises 9.9% YoY to $19.9m in H1 2025
data.description:
Revenue of the group also grew 15.7% YoY.

 

Frencken Group has reported a higher attributable profit of $19.9m in the first half of 2025 (H1 2025), up 9.9% from $18.1m recorded in the same period a year ago.

The group’s revenue increased by 15.7% year-on-year (YoY) to $431.4m in H1 2025.

The revenue growth is partially due to the improvements in the group’s Mechatronics Division, which saw revenue rise 19% YoY to $389.3m in H1 2025.

According to Frencken, this was driven by higher revenue contributions from the semiconductor, medical, and industrial automation segments, which offset a dip in revenue from the analytical life sciences segment.

For H1 2025, the semiconductor segment’s revenue grew 37.5% YoY to $215.7m. This was attributed mainly to stable sales growth to a key customer in Europe and a rebound in sales from the Asia operations.

data.title: Cordlife posts $4.6m net loss for H1 2025
data.description:
Revenue amounted to $19.2m for the period.

Cordlife Group Limited has incurred a net loss after tax of $4.6m for the six months ended 30 June (H1 2025).

The group’s H1 2025 revenue stood at $19.2m, up 108.8% from the previous year.

Gross profit increased by 680.7% to $10.9m during the period.

Selling and marketing expenses rose by 5.9% to $7.7m.

data.title: Alpina Holdings' net profit jumps 239.6% YoY to $2.7m in H1 2025
data.description:
It surpassed the $2.4m net profit of the company in FY2024.


Alpina Holdings saw its net profit surge to $2.7m in the first half of 2025 (H1 2025) from the $800,000 net profit reported in the same period the previous year.

It was a 239.6% year-on-year (YoY) increase, primarily attributed to a dip in administrative expenses and steady revenue growth.

According to Alpina, its H1 2025 net profit already surpassed the group’s net profit of $2.4m in FY2024.

The group recorded an 11% YoY decline in administrative expenses due to the absence in H1 2025 of the professional fees related to the acquisition of Wan Dormitory, as the transaction was completed in March 2024.

Alpina’s revenue for H1 2025 was $47.9m, up by 8.7% YoY. The revenue growth was driven primarily by improved performance of the group’s integrated building services segment and an increase in rental income.

The group also said that following the completion of its acquisition of a property situated at 180 Woodlands Industrial Park in March 2024, the group recognised a full six-month contribution of rental income in H1 2025, which increased to $2.3m, representing an increase of 87.8% as compared to the same period the previous year.

The group proposed an interim dividend of 1.0 Singapore cent per share, representing approximately 69.4% of the net profit attributable to shareholders in H1 2025 and marks the company’s largest single dividend payout since its listing in 2022.

data.title: Singlife partners CapBridge to offer life and health insurance
data.description:
This enables CapBridge’s financial advisers to provide Singlife policies.

Singlife has partnered with CapBridge, a digital investment platform under FOMO Group, to offer life and health insurance products to its clients. 

The move marks CapBridge’s entry into insurance distribution, following its recent expansion into insurance advisory under a Capital Markets Services licence.

The deal enables CapBridge’s financial advisers to provide Singlife policies alongside investment products such as money market funds, bonds, and ETFs. 

The offering will follow a needs-based assessment in line with regulations, targeting both individual and SME clients.

For Singlife, the partnership is its eighth ecosystem collaboration in 2025 and expands its SME reach through its SME Connect programme. 

Both companies said the tie-up supports their aim of integrating investment and insurance solutions within a single financial platform.

data.title: Dezign Format raises $6.5m from 32.5 million placement shares
data.description:
Trading of the shares commences on 15 August.

 

Dezign Format Group Limited raised gross proceeds of $6.5m from the placement of 32.5 million shares at $0.20 per placement share.

The shares are expected to commence trading on 15 August under the ticker symbol UZF.

Evolve Capital Advisory Private Limited is the sponsor, issue manager and placement agent for the listing and the placement.

Dezign Format said it attracted commitments from various institutional investor groups, including Asdew Acquisitions Pte Ltd, Lion Global Investors Limited, Maybank Asset Management Singapore Pte Ltd, Nikko Asset Management Asia Ltd, Pheim Asset Management (Asia) Pte Ltd, and Ginko-AGT Global Growth Fund.

The company’s post-IPO market capitalisation is $40m.

data.title: Great Eastern issues bonus shares with trading set to resume
data.description:
After the issue, GEH will have 503.03 million ordinary shares.

Great Eastern Holdings Limited (GEH) said shareholders have chosen to receive 443.61 million Class C non-voting shares and 29.71 million bonus ordinary shares from its planned bonus share issue of up to 473.32 million shares. 

The shares were given on a one-for-one basis to eligible shareholders.

After the issue, GEH will have 503.03 million ordinary shares and 443.61 million Class C non-voting shares. 

Public investors will own about 11.76% of the ordinary shares, meeting the Singapore Exchange’s requirement. 

Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation (OCBC) will hold 88.19% of the ordinary shares and all of the Class C shares.

With the public shareholding restored, GEH will ask for its trading suspension to be lifted. 

Trading is expected to restart on 21 August, when the bonus ordinary shares will also be listed on the SGX.

data.title: NeraTel reports $1.8m net loss for H1 2025
data.description:
This was due to foreign exchange adjustments and transitional restructuring costs.

 

 

Nera Telecommunications Ltd (NeraTel) has reported a revenue of $44.7m and a net loss of $1.8m for the first half of 2025.

According to its bourse filing, the net loss was largely attributable to foreign exchange adjustments and transitional restructuring costs.

Revenue also declined by 5.5% compared to the previous year. This was primarily due to a recalibration in the service provider segment, which saw a strategic shift in focus as part of ongoing efforts to diversify NeraTel’s segment mix and enhance long-term growth potential.

The group secured approximately $40.3m in order intake for H1 2025, a decrease of 47.5% as compared to $76.8m in H1 2024.

NeraTel's gross profit for H1 2025 was $9.3m, with the group maintaining a gross profit margin of 20.7%, demonstrating continued operational efficiency amid reduced operating expenditure.

The group said a notable achievement was the 20% reduction in operating expenses, driven by disciplined cost management across distribution, selling, and administrative functions, positioning the group for leaner and more agile operations.

data.title: Yanlord Land posts $68m profit in H1
data.description:
Revenue also fell 53.5% year-on-year to $1.66b.

Yanlord Land Group Limited reported a profit attributable to owners of the company of $67.7m (RMB379m) for the first half of 2025.

Revenue fell 53.5% year-on-year to $1.66b (RMB9.286b), with property development income down 59.9% to $1.25b (RMB7.012b) due to fewer projects delivered.

The company completed two projects during the period—Yanlord Four Seasons The Park 3 in Shenzhen and Yanlord Central Lake (Phase 3) in Taicang—which generated nearly 80% of property sales revenue.

Additionally, income from property investment and hotel operations rose 7.5% to $172m (RMB961m), and property management revenue increased 6.4% to $112m (RMB629m).

Other segments declined 30.1% to $122m (RMB685m).

data.title: Hotel Properties appoints architects for Orchard properties redevelopment
data.description:
The firms will submit revised redevelopment plans by 24 August.

Hotel Properties Limited (HPL) has appointed architectural firms Rogers Stirk Harbour + Partners (RSHP) and DP Architects (DPA) to lead the redevelopment of The Forum, voco Orchard Singapore, and HPL House.

RSHP, based in London, is known for innovative and sustainable designs worldwide. DPA, headquartered in Singapore, has delivered major projects such as The Esplanade – Theatres on the Bay and Dubai Mall.

The firms will submit revised redevelopment plans by 24 August 2025, following the Grant of Provisional Permission.

The submission will include detailed Building Information Modelling (BIM) data to facilitate planning review and coordination.

HPL said the revisions reflect ongoing consultations with authorities to align the project with market conditions and regulations.

data.title: Hotel Properties posts $11.4m half-year profit
data.description:
Revenue for the period rose 9% to $378.4m.

Hotel Properties Limited (HPL) posted a net profit of $11.4m for the half year ended 30 June 2025, reversing a $4.9m loss a year earlier.

Revenue rose 9% to $378.4m, driven by the opening of Four Seasons Hotel Osaka, whilst gross profit increased to $85.2m.

Operating profit before associates, depreciation, fair value changes, and finance costs grew 6.3% to $86.9m.

The group recorded a $9.3m fair value loss on long-term investments, and finance costs edged up to $51m.

Profits from associates and joint ventures rose to $7.7m, boosted by a gain from Paddington Square, London.

HPL completed the acquisition of the entire strata area of Concorde Hotel & Shopping Mall in Singapore on 11 August 2025, recording a $27.3m fair value gain on the shop units.
